title: Getting started with TestGenerator
text: Excel input file contain one sheet per OMOP CDM table. example, sheet names can include person, observation_period, visit_occurrence, condition_occurrence, drug_exposure, measurement. writes icu_sample.json output_path. Keeping JSON files tests/testthat/testCases makes easy reuse package tests. outputPath = NULL, TestGenerator writes default test case folder.
code: library(TestGenerator)  file_path <- system.file(   "extdata",   "icu_sample_population.xlsx",   package = "TestGenerator" )  output_path <- file.path(tempdir(), "testgenerator-example") dir.create(output_path, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)  readPatients(   filePath = file_path,   testName = "icu_sample",   outputPath = output_path,   cdmVersion = "5.4" )

what: Load the Test Population into a CDM
title: Getting started with TestGenerator
text: Use patientsCDM() create CDM reference containing small patient population complete vocabulary. default, CDM created DuckDB. pathJson = NULL, TestGenerator looks JSON files tests/testthat/testCases.
code: cdm <- patientsCDM(   pathJson = output_path,   testName = "icu_sample",   cdmVersion = "5.4" )  cdm[["person"]] cdm <- patientsCDM(   pathJson = NULL,   testName = "icu_sample",   cdmVersion = "5.4" )

what: Use the CDM in Unit Tests
title: Getting started with TestGenerator
text: test CDM available, run study code use real CDM. package includes example cohort definitions inst/extdata/test_cohorts. package test, place code tests/testthat/test-*.R assert specific counts, dates, durations, intersections study produce micro population.
code: library(CDMConnector) library(dplyr) library(testthat)  test_cohorts <- system.file(   "extdata",   "test_cohorts",   package = "TestGenerator" )  cohort_set <- readCohortSet(test_cohorts)  cdm <- generateCohortSet(   cdm = cdm,   cohortSet = cohort_set,   name = "test_cohorts" )  cohort_attrition <- attrition(cdm[["test_cohorts"]])  excluded_records <- cohort_attrition |>   pull(excluded_records) |>   sum()  expect_equal(excluded_records, 0)

what: Start from a Blank Excel Template
title: Getting started with TestGenerator
text: want design new test population scratch, create Excel workbook required CDM table columns. Fill workbook rows small set patients needed test, pass completed workbook readPatients().
code: generateTestTables(   tableNames = c(     "person",     "observation_period",     "visit_occurrence",     "condition_occurrence",     "drug_exposure",     "measurement"   ),   cdmVersion = "5.4",   outputFolder = output_path,   filename = "my_test_population" )

title: Getting started with TestGenerator
text: CSV inputs, place one file per CDM table folder. File names match table names, example person.csv observation_period.csv. source datasets large integer identifiers, set reduceLargeIds = TRUE.
code: csv_path <- system.file(   "extdata",   "mimic_sample",   package = "TestGenerator" )  readPatients.csv(   filePath = csv_path,   testName = "mimic_sample",   outputPath = output_path,   cdmVersion = "5.4" )

title: Getting started with TestGenerator
text: local DuckDB examples, disconnect test finished.
code: DBI::dbDisconnect(CDMConnector::cdmCon(cdm), shutdown = TRUE) unlink(output_path, recursive = TRUE)

title: GitHub Actions
text: Create supported workflows : create workflow need: function writes workflow files .github/workflows. Commit files repository GitHub Actions can run .
code: library(TestGenerator)  useGithubAction(overwrite = TRUE) useGithubAction(dbms_type = "postgresql", overwrite = TRUE) useGithubAction(dbms_type = "sqlserver", overwrite = TRUE) useGithubAction(dbms_type = "databricks", overwrite = TRUE)

title: GitHub Actions
text: workflow templates run fixed test file names. package must provide file backend enable: test file call package code, create test CDM patientsCDM(dbms = ...), run study logic, use testthat expectations check result. test error failed expectation, action intended fail.

title: GitHub Actions
text: PostgreSQL SQL Server, GitHub Actions workflow creates Docker container inside action run. means users need configure database secrets two workflows. action sets connection environment variables tests temporary container. makes two workflows useful routine CI, run starts fresh database service.

title: GitHub Actions
text: Databricks, action connects remote Databricks workspace. must configure required repository secrets GitHub: Optional Databricks secrets : Databricks uses real remote workspace, make sure token enough permission create schemas tables, insert test data, query result, remove test schema afterwards.

title: GitHub Actions
text: Start PostgreSQL SQL Server first, workflows create database containers require external credentials. Add Databricks confirmed remote workspace, token, HTTP path work GitHub Actions. Keep backend-specific tests small deterministic. good CI test load small Unit Test Definition, run one important part package logic, assert expected counts, dates, cohort records. release preparation, backend-specific workflows can also act quality gate. darwin-eu-dev/TestGenerator, CRAN-release-check workflow continues develop commit already successful runs R-CMD-check, R Package Tests (PostgreSQL), R Package Tests (Spark Databricks), R Package Tests (SQL Server). means CRAN checks run commit already tested supported database backends.

what: Use patientsCDM() with a remote database
title: Remote databases
text: First create Unit Test Definition JSON file readPatients() readPatients.csv(). call patientsCDM() backend want test. supported remote values dbms : remote databases, patientsCDM() creates CDM locally first, trims vocabulary test population needs, uploads result remote database, returns remote CDM reference. default, TestGenerator creates temporary test schema upload. can also provide writeSchema want choose schema name . test finished, clean remote schema close connection: important shared remote databases. test schemas small, cleaning keeps database tidy avoids name conflicts later runs.
code: library(TestGenerator)  cdm <- patientsCDM(   pathJson = "tests/testthat/testCases",   testName = "my_test_population",   cdmVersion = "5.4",   dbms = "postgresql" ) cdm <- patientsCDM(   pathJson = "tests/testthat/testCases",   testName = "my_test_population",   cdmVersion = "5.4",   dbms = "sqlserver",   writeSchema = "testgenerator_my_case" ) cleanupTestCdm(cdm)

title: Remote databases
text: call patientsCDM() directly remote database, TestGenerator reads connection details environment variables. Databricks, TestGenerator also reads DATABRICKS_USER DATABRICKS_WORKSPACE set. set, uses token user hive_metastore workspace/catalog. SQL Server, TestGenerator reads SQL_SERVER_DRIVER set. set, uses ODBC Driver 18 SQL Server. typical local .Renviron setup look like : Use values match database Databricks workspace. database user needs permission create schemas, create tables, insert data, read data, drop test schema cleanup.
code: POSTGRESQL_SERVER=localhost POSTGRESQL_DBNAME=cdm POSTGRESQL_PORT=5432 POSTGRESQL_USER=postgres POSTGRESQL_PASSWORD=your-password  SQLSERVER_SERVER=localhost SQLSERVER_DBNAME=cdm SQLSERVER_PORT=1433 SQLSERVER_USER=sa SQLSERVER_PASSWORD=your-password  DATABRICKS_HOST=https://your-workspace.cloud.databricks.com DATABRICKS_TOKEN=your-token DATABRICKS_HTTPPATH=/sql/1.0/warehouses/your-warehouse

title: Remote databases
text: backend-specific test usually follows shape: exact expectations specific micro population. Good tests usually check counts, dates, cohort entry exit, exclusions, result predictable small input dataset.
code: testthat::test_that("study logic works on PostgreSQL", {   cdm <- NULL   on.exit({     if (!is.null(cdm)) {       TestGenerator::cleanupTestCdm(cdm)     }   }, add = TRUE)    cdm <- TestGenerator::patientsCDM(     pathJson = "tests/testthat/testCases",     testName = "my_test_population",     cdmVersion = "5.4",     dbms = "postgresql"   )    result <- myPackage::runMyStudy(cdm)    testthat::expect_equal(result$n_subjects, 3) })

what: Integration Unit Tests for Pharmacoepidemiological Studies
title: Integration Unit Tests for Pharmacoepidemiological Studies
text: cohort pick correct number patients? calculating intersection right way? expected value treatment duration? takes one incorrect parameter get incoherent results pharmacoepidemiological study, testing calculations huge, complex databases challenging. TestGenerator useful: lets push small sample patients unit test study OMOP CDM. includes tools create blank CDM complete vocabulary check whether code expect specific cases. package based unit tests written Erasmus MC Ranitidine Study.

title: Integration Unit Tests for Pharmacoepidemiological Studies
text: Install released version CRAN:
code: install.packages("TestGenerator")

title: Integration Unit Tests for Pharmacoepidemiological Studies
text: TestGenerator starts small patient dataset. data can stored Excel workbook, one sheet per OMOP CDM table, folder CSV files, one file per table. help creating Excel input scratch, see Start Blank Excel Template section website. package converts files Unit Test Definition JSON file. JSON file object keep package tests. outputPath = NULL, JSON file written tests/testthat/testCases, usual location package tests. can also call Excel CSV readers directly:
code: TestGenerator::readPatients(   filePath = "inst/extdata/icu_sample_population.xlsx",   testName = "icu_sample",   outputPath = "tests/testthat/testCases",   cdmVersion = "5.4" ) TestGenerator::readPatients.xl(   filePath = "inst/extdata/icu_sample_population.xlsx",   testName = "icu_sample",   outputPath = "tests/testthat/testCases",   cdmVersion = "5.4" )  TestGenerator::readPatients.csv(   filePath = "inst/extdata/icu_sample_population_csv",   testName = "icu_sample",   outputPath = "tests/testthat/testCases",   cdmVersion = "5.4",   reduceLargeIds = FALSE )

title: Integration Unit Tests for Pharmacoepidemiological Studies
text: Use patientsCDM() load one Unit Test Definition blank OMOP CDM. default, creates local DuckDB CDM small patient population vocabulary needed testing. pathJson = NULL, TestGenerator looks JSON file tests/testthat/testCases. example uses sample ICU population included package.
code: cdm <- TestGenerator::patientsCDM(   pathJson = "tests/testthat/testCases",   testName = "icu_sample",   cdmVersion = "5.4" ) file_path <- system.file(   "extdata",   "icu_sample_population.xlsx",   package = "TestGenerator" )  output_path <- file.path(tempdir(), "testgenerator-example") dir.create(output_path, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)  TestGenerator::readPatients(   filePath = file_path,   testName = "icu_sample",   outputPath = output_path,   cdmVersion = "5.4" )  cdm <- TestGenerator::patientsCDM(   pathJson = output_path,   testName = "icu_sample",   cdmVersion = "5.4" )  DBI::dbDisconnect(CDMConnector::cdmCon(cdm), shutdown = TRUE) unlink(output_path, recursive = TRUE)

what: Use it in a package test
title: Integration Unit Tests for Pharmacoepidemiological Studies
text: useful pattern keep small JSON test case tests/testthat/testCases, build CDM inside testthat test, run study code, assert expected result. exact expectation come micro population designed. Good tests usually check subject counts, inclusion exclusion rules, cohort dates, treatment durations easy verify hand.
code: testthat::test_that("cohort construction returns the expected patients", {   cdm <- TestGenerator::patientsCDM(     pathJson = "tests/testthat/testCases",     testName = "icu_sample",     cdmVersion = "5.4"   )   withr::defer(     DBI::dbDisconnect(CDMConnector::cdmCon(cdm), shutdown = TRUE)   )    cohort_set <- CDMConnector::readCohortSet(     system.file("extdata", "test_cohorts", package = "TestGenerator")   )    cdm <- CDMConnector::generateCohortSet(     cdm = cdm,     cohortSet = cohort_set,     name = "test_cohorts"   )    result <- cdm[["test_cohorts"]] |>     dplyr::collect()    testthat::expect_equal(     sort(unique(result$subject_id)),     c(1, 2, 4, 5, 6, 7)   ) })

text: datasetName data set name found https://github.com/darwin-eu/EunomiaDatasets. data set name corresponds folder data set ZIP files cdmVersion OMOP CDM version. version appear suffix data file, example: synpuf_5.3.zip. Default: '5.3' pathToData path Eunomia data stored file system., default value environment variable "EUNOMIA_DATA_FOLDER" used. overwrite Control whether existing archive file overwritten already exist.

what: Pushes test population into a blank CDM. — patientsCDM
title: Pushes test population into a blank CDM. — patientsCDM
text: Pushes test population blank CDM.

title: Pushes test population into a blank CDM. — patientsCDM
code: patientsCDM(   pathJson = NULL,   testName = NULL,   cdmVersion = "5.4",   cdmName = NULL,   dbms = "duckdb",   writeSchema = NULL )

title: Pushes test population into a blank CDM. — patientsCDM
text: pathJson Directory sample populations json located. NULL, gets default inst/testCases directory. testName Name sample population JSON file. NULL push first sample population testCases directory. cdmVersion cdm version, default "5.4". cdmName Name cdm, default NULL. dbms Database management system use. One "duckdb", "databricks", "sqlserver" "postgresql". Default "duckdb" creates local DuckDB CDM. remote databases function creates CDM locally, trims vocabulary, uploads new schema remote database, returns remote CDM reference. Remote databases require environment variables set. Call usethis::edit_r_environ() set . writeSchema Optional schema name use remote database. NULL (default), unique schema created automatically. used dbms "duckdb".

title: Pushes test population into a blank CDM. — patientsCDM
text: CDM reference object sample population.

title: Pushes test population into a blank CDM. — patientsCDM
code: # \donttest{ filePath <- system.file("extdata", "testPatientsRSV.xlsx", package = "TestGenerator") TestGenerator::readPatients(filePath = filePath, outputPath = tempdir()) #> ✔ All tables are valid #> ✔ Unit Test Definition Created Successfully: 'test' cdm <- TestGenerator::patientsCDM(pathJson = tempdir(), testName = "test") #>  #> Download completed! #> Creating CDM database /tmp/RtmpXl4UQE/empty_cdm_5.4.zip #> ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■             65% | ETA:  2s #> ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■         81% | ETA:  3s #> ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■       86% | ETA:  2s #> ! cdm name not specified and could not be inferred from the cdm source table #> ✔ Standard table(s) in test data: person, observation_period, condition_occurrence, visit_occurrence, visit_detail and death #> ✔ Patients pushed to blank CDM successfully duckdb::duckdb_shutdown(duckdb::duckdb()) # }

what: Create GitHub Action workflows for specific DBMS — useGithubAction
title: Create GitHub Action workflows for specific DBMS — useGithubAction
text: Copies GitHub Action workflow templates package's inst/workflows directory .github/workflows directory current project. copied workflows can used run backend-specific tests PostgreSQL, SQL Server, Databricks.

title: Create GitHub Action workflows for specific DBMS — useGithubAction
code: useGithubAction(   dbms_type = c("postgresql", "sqlserver", "databricks"),   overwrite = FALSE )

title: Create GitHub Action workflows for specific DBMS — useGithubAction
text: dbms_type character vector supported DBMS types process. Supported values "postgresql", "sqlserver", "databricks". overwrite logical value indicating whether overwrite existing workflow files .github/workflows directory. Defaults FALSE.

title: Create GitHub Action workflows for specific DBMS — useGithubAction
text: Invisibly returns TRUE selected workflow files copied successfully.

title: Create GitHub Action workflows for specific DBMS — useGithubAction
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ useGithubAction(dbms_type = "postgresql") useGithubAction(   dbms_type = c("postgresql", "sqlserver", "databricks"),   overwrite = TRUE ) } # }

title: TestGenerator 0.8.0
text: Fixed missing cdm_schema attribute cdm_reference using remote databases (e.g., SQL Server). Implemented dynamic, unique schema names (prefixed cdm_testgenerator_) prevent database collisions. Fixed patientsCDM() ensure correct remote CDM reference returned injection process. Added regression tests verify cdm_schema attribute presence CohortConstructor compatibility SQL Server. Removed DARWIN_ prefix environment variables run tests SQL Server Postgres.

title: TestGenerator 0.7.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-30 Support PostgreSQL DBMS patientsCDM() Added RPostgres Imports Added skip_on_cran() several tests avoid network dependencies R CMD check Updated .gitignore .Rbuildignore

title: TestGenerator 0.3.3
text: CRAN release: 2024-11-08 generateTestTable() creates Excel file sheets correspond OMOP-CDM tables. patientsCDM() now accepts cdmName argument allow custom cdm name. getEmptyCDM() returns empty cdm object.

title: TestGenerator 0.3.1

JSONS saved testthat/testCases folder default better test self-containment. Tested MIMIC database.
